title: centaurea omphalodes
intro: Centaurea omphalodes, also known as kidney vetch or blue whortle, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region and is a member of the daisy family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and inflammation. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as kidney stones and urinary tract infections. Centaurea omphalodes is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
